At the Nangteshwar Shield tournament, Bogola and his teammates from the Sarbamangala Football Club face challenges not only on the field but also outside of it as the local club president strives to keep the ancestral shield in the family through hook or crook.

Amal, a quiet and simple man, dreams of breaking free from his routine life to chase hidden desires and temptations. As he steps out of his comfort zone, the journey tests his morals, courage, and longing for freedom. Will Amal dare to live the life he secretly desires?

Professor Gagan Sen (Uttam Kumar) takes on the challenge of transforming the spirited Sabitri (Moushumi Chatterjee) into a polished lady. What follows is a delightful comedy of manners, love, and misunderstandings, full of charm and classic Bengali humour.

In Kelor Kirti, three blissful couples—Guru (Dev) & Priya (Sayantika Banerjee), Joy (Jisshu Sengupta) & Rima (Mimi Chakraborty), and Apurba (Ankush) & Anushka (Koushani) — enjoy life until a mysterious woman, Urvashi (Nusrat Jahan), arrives. Amidst comedy and misunderstandings, secrets surface!

synopsis_text: In Baishe Srabon, directed by Srijit Mukherji, a serial killer terrorizes Kolkata, leaving behind Bengali poem verses as clues. Suspended cop Probir Roy Chowdhury (Prosenjit Chatterjee) and rookie Abhijit Pakrashi (Parambrata) team up to unravel the mystery, leading to a shocking revelation.

Jeet portrays Rohit, whose world turns upside down when he visits India with his son and meets his late wife’s twin sister, Rina (Koel Mallick). As they grow close, family tensions flare, questions of identity arise, and old secrets threaten their fragile bond.

In Raj Chakraborty’s Bojhena Shey Bojhena, two couples—Noor & Riya and Abhik & Joyeeta—find love and meaning in life. But a tragic bus accident intertwines their fates, revealing how unpredictable life can be and how a single moment can change everything.

Directed by Rituparno Ghosh, Chitrangada – The Crowning Wish explores the emotional journey of Rudra, a choreographer who decides to undergo gender reassignment surgery while staging Tagore’s Chitrangada. Rituparno, themself, acts in this film, turning it into one of their masterpieces.

Cholo Paltai follows the emotional journey of Gaurav, a boy who wants to be a cricketer, and his father, Shubhomoy, who pressures him to focus on academics. As conflicts rise, the film beautifully portrays their evolving bond and delivers a powerful message about understanding and support.

Directed by Aparna Sen, Goynar Baksho follows three generations of women—Rashmani (Moushumi Chatterjee), her niece-in-law Somalata (Konkona Sen Sharma), and granddaughter Chaitali (Srabanti)—bound by a haunted jewellery box. Guided by a witty ghost aunt, they face change and rebellion with humour.

synopsis_text: When three friends land in Bangkok to win over their girlfriends, nothing goes as planned. Mistaken identities, jealous lovers, and wild chases turn their romantic mission into utter madness. Packed with laughs, Jamai 420 features Ankush, Soham, Hiran, Paayel, Mimi, and Nusrat.

Akash (Dev) meets Barsha (Koel Mallick) and falls in love, only to learn she’s already engaged. As emotions deepen, Akash must find a way to win over her heart and her family. A romantic drama filled with charm and music, marked Dev and Koel’s second successful pairing.

Raj (Dev), a proud Bengali man, falls for Anna (Subhashree), an NRI pressured into an arranged marriage. When she plots to dissolve it by escaping to London, Raj follows her abroad. Amid chaos and cultural clashes, he challenges her illusions and fights for true love in this high-energy remake.

They weren’t like everyone else, but they found love in each other. As the world mocks and challenges their bond, the two must fight doubts, fears, and people who say they don’t belong. A heartfelt story of two misfits who dare to love, no matter how hard the world tries to pull them apart.

In Royal Bengal Rahasya, Feluda is invited to a forest estate by a Zamindar to crack a cryptic puzzle. But the visit takes a dark turn when a murder shocks everyone. Set against the backdrop of lush wilderness and looming secrets, Feluda must race against time to find the killer.

Begum Jaan, the fierce matriarch of a brothel, stands tall against the cruelty of a separatist state and the deep-rooted stigma surrounding her profession. As borders shift and threats grow, she and her girls refuse to bow, fighting for dignity, survival, and their right to exist.

While Detective Chandrakanta investigates the death of a man, which seems to be the result of a crime of passion, the man's much younger widow appears to be hiding something. What secrets are waiting to unfold?

When rising actress Doel Mitra’s name is dragged into a scandal, her world turns upside down. Thrust into the harsh glare of public judgment, she’s forced to confront society’s double standards on fame, morality, and a woman’s right to live on her own terms.

Shishir, an old man in his 70s, estranged from his family, unexpectedly bonds with four strangers. As they share stories, laughter, and pain, he rediscovers warmth, belonging, and the joy of human connection in the most unlikely company. A gentle tale of second chances and found family.

synopsis_text: After a tragic incident ends the life of theatre actress Kheya Chakraborty, people from different walks of life recount their memories of her. As each story reveals a new layer, Kheya’s life takes shape through love, ambition, heartbreak, and the shadows she left behind.

Ishaan is all logic, Khushi lives in dreams—two very different hearts with opposite takes on love. When their paths cross, emotions run high and conflicts rise. Can they bridge the gap between head and heart, or are they just too different to ever meet in the middle?
Based on the novel Srikanta of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay, the story revolves around Srikanta whose paths are crossed with Pyaribai as he discovers that she is his childhood love, Rajlakshmi.
The gruesome incident of Dhananjoy Chatterjee that almost shook the enitre nation, gets spoken of in this film adaptation. Watch the full movie on Hoichoi.
A struggling actor finally gets an audition, but chaos unfolds. Will this once-in-a-lifetime moment lead to the role of his dreams?

Three distinct tales brew together in this unique hoichoi Original, where love, loss, and laughter unfold over cups of Bengal’s beloved drink—chaa. Different lives, one emotion, and countless memories—all stirred by the magic of tea.

synopsis_text: As news of a meat scandal erupts, panic spreads across the city. But can society stomach the truth when corruption, commerce, and conscience collide?

synopsis_text: Illiteracy keeps Lubna from expressing her feelings for Rashed. Can love find its way when words fail and silence becomes the biggest barrier?
Enticed by the Blue Whale Challenge, a teenage girl spirals deeper into its deadly grip. As the final task looms, can she break free before it's too late?
Riju meets a girl who seems too perfect to be real. Is this chance encounter a coincidence, or something deeper pulling the strings?

While researching filmmaking, Shirin uncovers unsettling truths buried in the past. Just when the journey grows darker, she meets Arijit—an encounter that changes everything and leads her deeper into a story she never meant to tell.

Investigative journalist Shivangi arrives in misty Kurseong to find a missing girl. But the girl's cellphone begins to reveal eerie, supernatural clues—pulling Shivangi into a world where the living and the dead share secrets no one was meant to uncover.

What happens when three women hit pause on life and take a trip together? In Aami Aar Amar Girlfriends, Raima Sen, Swastika Mukherjee, and Parno Mittra play friends who escape the noise of everyday chaos to laugh, heal, and rediscover themselves in this breezy tale of bonding and self-love.

Joy and Jonaki are in love, but her strict father stands firmly against their relationship because of Joy’s poor academic record. As they try to win him over, love, determination, and a few surprises come into play. Will they succeed, or will failure write their fate?

Noor and Jahaan fall deeply in love, but their bond is tested when rising political tensions and communal unrest begin to threaten their lives. As love and hatred collide, they must fight to hold on to each other in a world being torn apart by division and violence.

Raja is a lovable slacker with zero direction—until Isha throws him a challenge: get serious about life or lose her forever! Armed with wild plans and zero skills, Raja sets out to prove himself. Will he rise to the task or crash hilariously? A fun-filled ride of love, laughs, and chaos!

A passionate filmmaker is desperate to break the mold with a truly original story. But as he hunts for the perfect plot, reality and imagination blur in unexpected ways. Will he find the story he’s looking for—or will the story find him?
A seasoned hitman is on his way to claim his dues when ghosts from his past resurface. Can he outrun the weight of guilt and the consequences of his choices?

After his epic journey in Chander Pahar, Shankar (Dev) returns for a new adventure in Amazon Obhijaan. He joins Marco and Anna on a thrilling quest through the Amazon jungle to find El Dorado—facing wild creatures, ancient secrets, and danger at every turn!
Aalo is a tender tale of love that defies uncertainty. Based on true events, it follows two hearts determined to bloom amidst life’s darkest odds.
What begins as a romantic evening takes a sharp turn when a game of secrets unearths buried truths. How well do they really know each other?

In this hoichoi adaptation of Tagore’s Laboratory, a determined widow fights to preserve her late husband’s lab while also seeking a suitable match for her daughter. Starring Anirban Bhattacharya and Darshana Banik, this powerful tale explores ambition, love, and a woman’s quiet strength.
Trapped in a world of betrayal, Junaid and Jatin discover that revenge is the only currency left in their shattered reality.

Shyamal's rising ambition takes a toll on his marriage with Charu, creating emotional distance and silent wounds. As their bond weakens, questions of love, faith, and sacrifice arise. Can they find their way back to each other, or has success cost them too much?
producer_names: ["Globsyn Media Ventures", "Cinemine Entertainment Pvt Ltd", "Maple Productions"]

synopsis_text: Rahul and Trina are opposites who spark a whirlwind romance full of highs, lows, and unexpected turns. As love clashes with reality, can they hold on to each other? A heartfelt hoichoi Exclusive directed by Mainak Bhaumik.

In Srijit Mukherji’s Yeti Obhijaan, the second Kakababu film after Mishawr Rawhoshyo, Kakababu and Santu journey to the Himalayas to investigate Yeti sightings. As myth meets mystery, their adventure unfolds through danger, deception, and thrilling discoveries in the snow-capped heights.

synopsis_text: In a world where biases run deep, Kali must confront discrimination and societal stigma to carve a space for herself and her dreams.
A forest officer leaves his family behind in a bungalow. But eerie events soon reveal they’re not as alone as they thought.
Set in 2027, after a devastating world war, a girl struggles to survive a seemingly normal life where the real scars lie hidden beneath.
After catching his wife with another man, Soumya seeks revenge. But can he control a game driven by rage, deception, and shattered trust?

Aniket (Abir Chatterjee) plans to shut down his late father’s ghost magazine—until real hauntings at a village school pull him into a chilling mystery. With Nandini (Sohini Sarkar), who claims to see spirits, he must face what he never believed in: the supernatural.
Lost in his own fantasies, Aki believes he has supernatural powers. But will imagination be enough to conquer the real-life bully tormenting him?
In search of a home, a woman steps into a vacant flat—unaware she may not be alone. What lurks behind closed doors?
A village girl dares to dream beyond societal chains. This acclaimed short captures her fight to be seen, heard, and remembered.
An elderly couple clings to hope as they wait year after year for their son’s visit. Will time bring reunion—or more silence?

In this quirky hoichoi Original, a shy man finds himself hopelessly smitten by his charming colleague. But as his nervousness spirals into strange behaviour, the question arises—are these just love jitters, or is something deeper going on?
While Radhika is on the brink of divorce, her husband dies suddenly. This incident takes her on a journey of reminiscence where she strives to know the unknown from her past.

Two completely different individuals fall in love, drawn together by fate despite their contrasting worlds. As life throws challenges their way, their bond is tested by hardships, choices, and time. A touching story of love, resilience, and the strength to hold on when everything falls apart.

A once-wealthy family now living in their 200-year-old ancestral home begins to face eerie and unexplained events. As fear and curiosity grow, they question whether these strange happenings are blessings in disguise or signs of something sinister. What secrets does the old house hold?

An army captain is framed and killed by those he trusted most. Branded a traitor, his honor is buried with him. Years later, his sons rise—not just to avenge, but to reclaim their father’s dignity. What follows is a tense reckoning with the past, marked by truth, redemption, and resolve.

They gave up everything for others—dreams, comfort, even stability. But when life turns its back and leaves them homeless, a couple must face a world that no longer remembers their kindness. A moving tale of love, loss, and the quiet strength to start over from nothing.
Aditya’s best friend Prachi is his business partner. He meets Esha and is smitten by her, being unaware about her past. Watch the full movie on Hoichoi.

Neeta devotes her life to supporting her family, giving up her dreams, happiness, and even her health. Yet, her sacrifices go unnoticed, and her worth remains unacknowledged. A poignant story of silent strength, emotional neglect, and the quiet pain of being taken for granted.

Based on Hindu mythology, this epic drama brings to life the fierce war between gods and demons, who descend to Earth to spread fear and chaos. As divine forces clash with darkness, the battle for balance, justice, and the fate of humanity unfolds in a tale of power, faith, and destiny.

This is the story of a man who takes on all sorts of odd jobs just to support his sister. Through poverty, struggle, and hardship, he never gives up. His journey is one of quiet strength, where family comes first and every sacrifice brings him closer to purpose.
The sole dream of Ramu and Uma to build a happy life together is burdened by a pile of obstacles on their way due to economic upheaval in post-partition India.

When young Gogol arrives at a friend’s home for Durga Puja, he discovers a precious statuette has gone missing. With the help of Ashok, he dives into a mystery full of clues, suspicion, and festive chaos. Can the duo crack the case before the celebrations end? A thrilling Puja-time whodunit!

Debdoot, an honest truck driver, lives a simple life with his two younger brothers. When they fall into the trap of a deceitful businessman, he’s forced to rise against the odds. What follows is a tense battle where loyalty is tested and survival becomes the only goal.

This quirky film reimagines the legendary lives of the Pandavas with a comic twist, blending mythology with modern humour. As epic moments turn hilarious, familiar tales are retold with satire, surprises, and a fresh take on the timeless Mahabharata saga.

An honest cop finds himself entangled with a web of corrupt politicians who try to manipulate the system for their gain. Refusing to give in, he takes a bold stand, turning the tables on those in power in a high-stakes battle against deceit and betrayal.

Aghat follows Subho, who loses his innocence after witnessing his mother’s brutal murder. Driven by rage, he avenges her death as a child—but the trauma transforms him into a hardened man. As his past haunts him, Subho battles with anger, guilt, and the search for redemption

In the first instalment of the Goenda Shabor series, Detective Shabor Dasgupta investigates the mysterious murder of Mitali, a young woman plagued by her messy past. Shabor and his assistant, Nanda, encounter a string of Mitali's friends and family who all turn out to be suspects in her murder.

Ria, a fierce male-basher, shocks everyone by marrying the gentle Deb. But on the very next morning, Deb is found dead! From newlywed to murder suspect overnight, Ria’s life turns into a wild mess of nosy relatives, clueless cops, and hilarious chaos. Did fate have other plans for her after all?

In One, IPS officer Ronojoy Bose suspects renowned scientist Aditya Sen, played by Prosenjit Chatterjee, of running a secret criminal empire. As Ronojoy digs deeper, a tense battle of wits begins. Can justice prevail against a mind that controls everything from the shadows?

Chaos erupts when an ageing woman drops a bombshell—she's marrying her much younger secret lover! Her shocked family spirals into drama, gossip, and hilarious attempts to “fix” her. A cheeky, heartwarming tale of love, age and family madness unfolds with flair!
producer_names: ["Paradise Film Production Company Limited Kan Singh Sodha"]

synopsis_text: Tomake Chai follows Joy and Diya, two star-crossed lovers whose bond is tested by time, fate, and life's unexpected turns. Despite separation, misunderstandings, and emotional hurdles, their love refuses to fade. Will destiny reunite them, or is their story one of love lost forever?

Kumar is sent to the city by his father for an important project, unaware that his assigned co-partner has a sinister plan to kill him. As danger closes in, Kumar must rely on his wit and courage to uncover the truth and escape a trap set by greed and ambition.

This is a heartfelt tale of a brother and sister whose strong bond is shaken by misunderstandings and hurt feelings. As emotions run high and distance grows, they must find a way to heal the rift. Will their love for each other be enough to overcome the pain and bring them back together?

After Raja walks out of Subhra’s life, Rajiv steps in and offers her a fresh start through marriage. Just as life begins to settle, Raja returns, bringing chaos and confusion. Caught between past love and present security, Subhra must make a difficult choice. Who will win her heart?

Sashibhushan, a strict and traditional patriarch, struggles to keep up with the changing mindset of the younger generation. As the youth around him seek freedom and independence, his rigid beliefs are challenged, leading to clashes, reflection, and the hope for understanding.

This screen adaptation of the Ramayana retells the epic through the eyes of Sita, Lord Rama’s devoted wife. Focusing on her trials, exile, and emotional turmoil, the film highlights Sita’s strength, sacrifice, and the often-overlooked tragedy behind her divine journey.

In this feel-good tale, Rik and Diya meet as strangers, unaware of their shared past. After a series of lighthearted events, Rik visits Diya’s home—only to discover she’s his long-lost childhood friend. What follows is a heartwarming journey of rediscovered friendship and old memories.

In Swarna Sikhar Prangane, a journey to Darjeeling becomes more than just a trip—it becomes a path to self-discovery. As a group of people travel together, they find answers to questions long buried, forming emotional bonds and uncovering memories that change their lives.

A young man falls for his charming new neighbour, who lives under the watchful eyes of her uncle and his ever-suspicious wife. As love quietly blooms across balconies and brief encounters, obstacles grow. Will their budding romance survive the scrutiny and find a happy ending?

Saat Pake Bandha stars Suchitra Sen as Archana and Soumitra Chatterjee as Sukhendu, a couple from different social classes whose marriage crumbles under pressure from Archana's disapproving mother? They must fight for their love, but will society let them write their own story?

The film tells the tragic tale of Kripa Sindhu and Sita, whose lives take a dark turn after they are cast out from their town. Faced with shame, struggle, and heartbreak, they must find the strength to survive. Can they overcome their past and start anew, or will fate strike again?

Looking for shelter, Bibhas takes refuge with a well-known village doctor. But soon, he senses something is wrong. The doctor’s kind behaviour hides a dark side. As strange things start to happen, Bibhas becomes worried. Can he find out the truth—or will he be trapped in danger?

Subrata joins his father’s business, only to walk away after clashing over values and principles. Torn between family duty and personal belief, he sets out on his own path. But in a world driven by power and profit, what challenges—and revelations—await his uncertain future?

A hilarious comedy about wealthy industrialist Biren Mishra's sons-in-law, Arvind, Hadu and Raja. The first two live-off from Mishra's property until petty thief Raja enters their family, as the youngest son-in-law and turns the tables.

This film captures the charm of young love between Billu, a simple and kind-hearted boy, and Swati, the spirited sister of a local gangster. As their bond deepens, they must face fear, family pressure, and danger—making their love story both tender and full of unexpected turns.

After his wife's death, Kolkata detective Aniket Sanyal takes a long vacation to heal. But peace is short-lived when a tourist is found dead under mysterious circumstances. Drawn into the case, Aniket must uncover the truth, turning his escape into a gripping investigation.

A close bond between a brother and his sister begins to fall apart when she falls in love, against his wishes. Hurt and conflicted, he distances himself, while she struggles to make peace between love and family. Can she mend their bond and follow her heart at the same time?

When her innocent husband is falsely accused of murder, a poor and helpless woman is pushed to the edge. With no support and a broken world around her, she takes justice into her own hands. As she fights against power and prejudice, the question remains—will she succeed in clearing his name?

Kiran’s world changes when she gives birth to a baby with a disability. People doubt her, and life gets tough, but Kiran never gives up. With strong faith and a brave heart, she faces every problem, proving that love and hope can help her overcome even the hardest times.

Set in a time when life moved slower and love was shy and tender, the film tells a gentle story of first love. With stolen glances, quiet moments, and unspoken feelings, it explores the joy and pain of new emotions. A heartfelt journey through the innocence of young hearts.

Ghetu is always made fun of for her dark skin and is compared to her pretty sister Malika. Everyone praises Malika, while Ghetu is left behind, feeling hurt and unwanted. But deep inside, she hopes for a better life. Will her days of sadness end, or will she always live in her sister’s shadow?

Set in rural Bengal, the film explores a time when rigid customs and social taboos ruled life. Amidst this, two lovers find themselves trapped between love and tradition. As they struggle against societal norms, their tale becomes one of heartbreak, resistance, and doomed longing.

Amitabha and Lajwanti live simple lives until they cross paths with Kumar Saheb, a powerful local smuggler. What starts as a chance meeting soon pulls them into danger. As trouble grows, their lives spiral out of control in ways they never imagined.

In Rituparno Ghosh's final film, Satyanweshi, detective Byomkesh Bakshi (Sujoy Ghosh) probes the mysterious disappearance of a palace librarian in Balabantpur. As he delves deeper, hidden truths about the royal family's secrets and ambitions emerge, leading to a gripping revelation.

Awara follows Surya Narayan, who leaves his IT job to help a friend and moves to Cooch Behar. He falls for Poulomi at a fair, only to clash with local thug Shanatan Panja. When trouble follows her to Kolkata, Surya must fight a powerful gangster to protect love and family.

This mythological tale follows Goddess Manasa’s longing to be worshipped like other deities. But when her wish is denied, her wrath strikes Lakhindar, son of Chand Sadagar. His new bride, Behula, embarks on a heart-wrenching journey of faith and resilience, seeking to defy fate and restore love.

Srijit Mukherji’s debut film Autograph follows young director Shuvobrata as he remakes Ray’s Nayak with superstar Arun and his girlfriend Srinandita in lead roles. But as the lines between fiction and reality blur, the trio is drawn into a tangled web of ambition, love, and betrayal.

Directed by Raj Chakraborty, Challenge tells the story of Abir (Dev), who joins college and wins the heart of Pooja (Subhashree Ganguly), daughter of a powerful don. As he faces threats and challenges, Abir must prove his love and bravery in this high-stakes romantic drama.
Khoka fights for his friend Shibu, a trapeze artist who is not provided adequate compensation after he meets with an accident. Things get complicated when Khoka falls for Shibu's daughter.

Gita tries to reconnect with her estranged father, who remains distant as he awaits the return of his younger daughter from America. As emotions run high and long-held grievances resurface, the family navigates a delicate path toward reconciliation and understanding.

An aspiring footballer struggles against poverty, pressure, and self-doubt on his path to success. Just as the odds stack up, his elder brother steps in, reigniting hope and pushing him toward his dream. In the game of life, sometimes your biggest win is having someone believe in you.
Simpleton Madhu believes nobody can harm him as long as his mother’s blessings are on him. Will his belief stay deep rooted? Watch the full movie on Hoichoi.

MLA Fatakesto follows the journey of an ex-anti-social turned fiery politician who stands up for justice with his iconic one-liners. Played by Mithun Chakraborty, Fatakesto is bold, moral, and fearless. This family entertainer unfolds over a week of chaos, conflict, and larger-than-life heroism.

Rishi enters a chaotic household to unite Sulochona and Sashibhushan, ex-lovers torn apart 15 years ago by ego and pride. But amid the reunion mission, he finds himself falling for their daughter Rhea. Nater Guru, starring Jeet and Koel Mallick, is their first romantic blockbuster.

While camping in a forest, Raja and his friends discover a wounded child and uncover a shocking kidnapping plot. As they try to rescue him, they’re drawn into a dangerous chase against ruthless criminals. A gripping tale of courage, friendship, and doing what’s right—no matter the cost.

Set against the backdrop of the Partition, the film explores the sorrow of being torn away from one’s motherland. Through the eyes of women, it redefines the meaning of ‘freedom’—not just as a national right, but as a deeply personal journey of identity, dignity, and the fight to belong.

In Obhishopto Nighty, directed by Birsa Dasgupta, the snake-charmer’s bar singer Monica is killed and her spirit gets trapped in a pink lacy nighty. Whoever wears it becomes the target of her ghostly vengeance. Blending comedy and horror, this quirky fantasy-thriller haunts with laughs and chills.

A projectionist, a fugitive driver, and a pregnant refugee journey across rural Bengal—each chasing a fragile dream. As their paths entwine, harsh realities threaten to derail hope. Swapnerdin is a haunting tale of longing, resilience, and the dreams we refuse to let go.

Deb (Soham), a city-slicker, pretends to be a humble villager to win over Rani (Srabanti), the daughter of a strict rural family. But when his lies begin to trip over each other, Deb must either come clean or keep juggling the madness—all for love, of course!

Pran dreams of building a school in his village to give children a better future. Battling poverty, politics, and resistance, he finally sees his dream come true. But just as hope blooms, a shocking revelation threatens to shake everything he’s built. Can he protect what he started?

Rahul (Prosenjit Chatterjee) falls for Pallabi (Rituparna Sengupta), but her powerful family stands in the way. In disguise, he enters her home to win hearts and her hand. Directed by Haranath Chakraborty, Sasurbari Zindabad became the biggest Bengali blockbuster of 2000 and cemented a hit pairing.

Animesh, settled abroad, shares a deep bond with Ishita. But when unforeseen troubles shake their world, doubts and distance creep in. Caught between love and chaos, the couple must fight emotional battles and outside forces to see if their love can truly stand the test of time.

Sedin Dekha Hoyechilo shows a strict father played by Tapas Paul, who hunts down his elder daughter’s eloping fiancé. Among those captured is Abir (Dev), who unexpectedly falls for the younger sister Nandini (Srabanti). Amidst search and family drama, love blossoms in the most unlikely place.

Raja Birchandra shows little interest in ruling his kingdom, lost in his own carefree world. Taking advantage of his indifference, some villagers misuse power for personal gain. As things slowly spiral out of control, will the king wake up to the truth and take charge before it’s too late?

Raju, wealthy and well-settled, meets Kashinath, a poor but kind-hearted man. Despite their differences, they form an unlikely bond. But as life takes unexpected turns, jealousy, pride, and harsh realities test their friendship. Will fate reunite them or leave them worlds apart forever?

This entertaining rom-com follows Madhusudan, a straightforward Bengali man with an unusual dream—he wants to marry a wealthy Canadian woman. What follows is a hilarious journey of misadventures, culture clashes, and heartwarming twists as he chases love and ambition in equal measure.

In this Bengali classic, Bhanu Bandyopadhyay plays an artisan whose simple life takes a hilarious turn after he unexpectedly wins a lottery. As fortune knocks on his door, chaos, comedy, and confusion follow, testing friendships and values in this heartwarming tale of sudden riches.

After their father’s killer resurfaces and threatens their lives, three estranged siblings come together, bound by pain and purpose. As they confront past wounds and deadly enemies, the path to justice tests their unity, courage, and the strength of their bond.

In Beder Meye Josna, snake-catcher Josna saves Prince Anwar from a venomous snake. Grateful, the prince offers her any wish—she asks for his hand in marriage. As royalty resists, true love must challenge tradition and power.

Bedenir Prem tells the heartfelt story of a Bedeni girl, Josna (Anju Ghosh), who falls in love with Shiba (Chiranjeet), a boy adopted by snake-charmers. As their bond deepens, Shiba discovers his true family, forcing them to choose between love and identity in this drama of belonging and heart.

One of Hemen Gupta’s finest films, it unfolds against the backdrop of 1942’s political turmoil in Midnapore. As India’s freedom movement gains momentum, the story captures the fiery spirit of rebellion, the sacrifices made, and the emotional toll of resisting colonial oppression.

Abhi falls for Kajol, but their families are sworn enemies. Determined to end the blood feud, he enters rival territory with nothing but love and wit. Bindaas, starring Dev, Srabanti, and Sayantika, is a high-energy mix of action, romance, and emotions!

Joy is passionate about bikes and deeply in love with Nandini. But chasing dreams isn’t easy—life throws unexpected turns that test his spirit and resolve. As he races toward his goals, Joy must learn that love, ambition, and determination all demand more than just speed.
Clerk is a take at multiple personality disorders. The protagonist is a clerk by day, but at night his alter ego emerges. Watch the full movie on Hoichoi.

Bhanu arrives in Kolkata with dreams of a better life and falls in love with Jyoti, a housemaid. But both are too young and naive to understand the hardships life has in store. As reality sets in, their innocent love story takes a painful turn down a road filled with struggles and loss.

In Chirodini Tumi Je Amar, directed by Raj Chakraborty, rich schoolgirl Pallavi (Priyanka Sarkar) falls for scooter mechanic Krishna (Rahul Banerjee). They elope, but her powerful family tracks them down, brutally separates them, and Krishna slips into madness, ending in tragedy and loss.

Based on Tagore’s work, this film explores how love and idealism collide during British rule. As revolution brews, characters must choose between emotion and duty. It highlights the cost of resistance, and how even the noblest ideals can lead to heartbreak in the fight for freedom.

Raja, a carefree and fun-loving young man, is forced to step up when his brother is injured before a major kickboxing match. With little training but a big heart, Raja enters the ring to fight in his brother’s place, facing pain, pressure, and pride to prove his love and strength for his family.

Challenge 2 is about Abhiraj Roy (Dev), a brave cop who wants to stop a dangerous don. To do so, he goes undercover and even becomes an MLA like his father. Packed with action, emotion, and drama, the film shows how far a son will go to fulfill his father’s dream.

With streaming platforms and multiplexes taking over, Kamalini—a beloved single-screen theatre in a small town—struggles to survive. As its owner and staff battle to keep the reels running, the film becomes a moving tribute to cinema, nostalgia, and the fading magic of the big screen.

Chopper captures the daily grind and hardships of the working class, painting a raw and emotional portrait of survival. Amidst this struggle, Rajat and Rina find unexpected comfort in each other. Their growing love becomes a rare spark of hope in an otherwise bleak world.

Four strangers get entangled with the underworld, each drawn in by desperation, greed, or chance. As they dive deeper, trust breaks down and betrayal lurks at every turn. A gritty thriller that unravels the dark maze of crime, where every choice could be their last.
Detective Shabor Dasgupta and his assistant, Nanda investigate the murder of a young woman and dive deep into the complexity of human psyche to catch the killer.
actor_names: ["Saswata Chatterjee", "Anirban Bhattacharya", "Jaya Ahsan", "Subhrajit Dutta", "Gaurav Chakrabarty", "Paayel Sarkar"]
_actor: ['Saswata Chatterjee', 'Anirban Bhattacharya', 'Jaya Ahsan', 'Subhrajit Dutta', 'Gaurav Chakrabarty', 'Paayel Sarkar']

synopsis_text: This film is a breath of fresh air, capturing the struggles, confusion, and emotional rollercoaster of student life. As young minds battle pressure, expectations, and identity crises, the story sheds light on the real challenges faced in academics and growing up!

Rahul (Jeet), a wealthy NRI, returns to Kolkata and meets Joy (Dev), a fearless youth, and Nandini (Koel Mallick), the woman they both love. Dui Prithibi, directed by Raj Chakraborty, marks the only on-screen pairing of Dev and Jeet in a powerful tale of love, conflict, and collision of worlds.

Amina and Abed are uprooted from their peaceful village and pushed into the chaos of city life by harsh circumstances. As they try to adjust, the shift takes a heavy toll on their emotions, values, and relationships. What begins as a move for survival slowly turns into a test of endurance.

A young couple deeply in love dreams of building a life together. But their journey hits a roadblock when both families oppose the match. Faced with emotional hurdles and generational divides, they must find a way to win over their loved ones and prove that love deserves a fair chance.

Bhawani Chatterjee, a flashy businessman, offers Mamta a job, but it doesn’t end with files and files only. Soon, he asks her for a “little favour” that sounds more dramatic than her favourite serial. With wit and sass, Mamta must figure out if she’s in an office or a comedy of errors!

In this hilarious tale of love and confusion, two best friends unknowingly fall for each other’s sisters. A series of mistaken identities, miscommunications, and family chaos follow—creating a rollercoaster of laughter and romance. Will love find a way amidst all the comic mayhem?

Go For Goals is a heartwarming sports drama that celebrates Bengal’s passion for football. Five inseparable friends whose lives revolve around the game. As they chase dreams and tackle personal struggles, football becomes their escape, their bond, and their ultimate test of loyalty.

The story unfolds around two rival gangs locked in constant conflict. As clashes intensify and tempers flare, the feud spirals beyond control. With loyalties tested and emotions high, the stage is set for a dramatic showdown. But who will emerge victorious—and at what cost?

A film by Srijit Mukherji, Hemlock Society stars Koel Mallick as Meghna, a heartbroken woman contemplating suicide. She meets Ananda Kar (Parambrata Chattopadhyay), who runs a strange institution that trains people in the art of dying. But in teaching death, he helps her choose life.

Haripada, an ordinary man, is kidnapped by a dangerous criminal. To survive, he takes on a false identity, stepping into a life full of lies, fear, and unexpected twists. As he gets pulled deeper into a risky game, one question remains—can he escape, or has fate written a new story for him?

Detective Byomkesh Bakshi travels to Varanasi to investigate the mysterious death of Deepnarayan. While everyone believes it to be suicide, Byomkesh senses something more sinister. As he digs deeper, hidden motives and dark secrets begin to unravel in this gripping whodunit.

Indra arrives in the city to spend time with his friend Rajib but finds his heart drawn to Rajib’s sister, Anu. As they grow closer, Indra struggles with his feelings, fearing they may hurt their bond. Will he take the leap and confess his love, or stay silent and let her slip away?

Rudra, a brave warrior, is torn apart from his love, Durga. Centuries later, they are reborn as Abir and Nandini. Drawn to each other once more, their past lives echo through time. As destiny unfolds, will this life give them the love they were once denied? A tale of timeless romance.

Once inseparable, two friends become bitter enemies, dividing their families for generations. Years later, their grandchildren cross paths—unaware of the past that binds them. As love and friendship blossom, they must decide: will they carry forward the hatred, or finally break the cycle?

Dr. Subhankar joins a neglected village hospital, bringing hope to a community long forgotten. As he heals with compassion and challenges deep-rooted corruption, resistance grows stronger. Can he fight the odds and stay true to his mission, or will the system force him to give up the fight?

Arka and Dodo participate in a reality show with false identities to blend into their surroundings and find themselves in a complex situation that will change their lives forever. Celebrate the spirit of friendship with Jackpot!

Rahul, a modern guy from the city, crosses paths with Pooja, a traditional girl rooted in simplicity. Despite their contrasting worlds, love blossoms between them. As their bond grows, they break social stereotypes and prove that love knows no boundaries. A sweet story of opposites attracting.

On a lonely night, an ageing actress prepares to end her life, revisiting memories of lost love, ambition, and regret. As she writes her final letter, the lines between her reel and real selves blur. Iti Mrinalini stars Aparna Sen and Konkona Sen Sharma in a deeply personal, reflective drama.

Inspired by Erich Segal’s Love Story, Love follows Rahul and Ria—two young hearts from starkly different worlds. As they fall deeply for each other, cultural gaps and family pressure threaten to tear them apart. But when life throws an even harsher twist, love becomes their only anchor.

DSP Bijoy Mukherjee, known for his honesty and courage, is devastated by his brother’s murder. Determined to bring the killers to justice, he takes on a powerful nexus of crime and corruption. As the stakes rise, Bijoy must risk everything to fulfill his mission—and his promise for justice.

Sonali and Subho are complete opposites sharing a shoebox flat for the cheap rent. From fridge wars to loud snoring, every day is a new battlefield. But amid the madness, something unexpected brews. Will they bury the hatchet or just keep hiding each other’s socks for revenge?

synopsis_text: Jeet plays Jeet, a man who returns to his village only to find it under the grip of crime and corruption. As he takes on the powerful to protect the innocent, he also tries to rekindle his bond with Rai (Swastika Mukherjee).

Anjali asks for a divorce from Akash on their wedding night, but the court asks the couple to stay together for six months before ending the marriage. In the course of these few months, Anjali ends up falling in love with Akash, but the way to Akash's heart is not so simple anymore.

After surviving a near-fatal bus accident, a group of strangers is shaken to the core. As they recover, each begins to experience strange, spiritual shifts—blurring the line between the seen and unseen. What begins as survival soon turns into a journey of awakening, reflection, and reckoning.

Shibu’s life changes when he meets Pallabi and falls in love with her. But soon, he discovers she’s dealing with serious problems. Determined to help, Shibu steps in to support her. As their bond grows, he must find the courage and wisdom to solve her troubles and win her love.

Sumanta (Rahul Bose) is stuck in a crumbling marriage. When he meets his estranged father (Mithun Chakraborty) after years, memories resurface. As silence gives way to truth, Kalpurush, also starring Sameera Reddy, unfolds as a quiet meditation on time and forgiveness.

A young couple deeply in love dreams of a future together, but their families strongly oppose the match. Faced with resistance, they must navigate emotional hurdles, generational conflicts, and personal sacrifices to prove their love. Will they manage to win over their families and unite?

Based on the works of Sunil Gangopadhyay, Mishawr Rahasya explores the story of Al Mamun, an Egyptian business tycoon, who asks Kakababu to help him decode some hieroglyphic symbols that will help trace the will of his teacher.

This comic-political drama follows the journey of a well-meaning politician, played by Mithun Chakraborty, who genuinely wants to bring change. But his honesty lands him in hilarious and chaotic situations as he battles corrupt rivals, public pressure, and twists in his fight to do what's right!

Memories in March showcases Deepti Naval as Arati, a grieving mother who comes to Kolkata after her son Siddhartha’s death. There, she meets Ornub (Rituparno Ghosh), his close friend, and discovers a side of her son she never knew. Raima Sen plays Siddhartha’s colleague.

Sagar (Prosenjit Chatterjee) and Shipra (Rituparna Sengupta) lose their baby in a hospital fire. Years later, love and tragedy reveal the truth about Maya (Srabanti Chatterjee) and Trishna in a moving family drama.

Based on the life of Ritwik Ghatak, Meghe Dhaka Tara tells the story of Nilkantha Bagchi, a filmmaker in a mental asylum. Haunted by Partition, personal loss, and creative struggles, he stages a play to reclaim his voice, reflecting an artist’s fight against despair and silence.

Raju Babu, scarred by a harsh past, finds direction in the guidance of his foster father and walks the same path to secure a better future. But the road he chooses comes with its own price—turning his quest for peace into a storm of power, loyalty, and buried truths.

Set against a backdrop of inequality, the film portrays the lives of people from varied social and economic backgrounds. As their paths intersect, it sheds light on the hardships they face, the resilience they show, and the hopes they cling to in a society marked by deep divides.

Tia is in love with Sourav, but the relatives who raised her strongly disapprove of their relationship. As emotions run high and tensions grow, the couple must navigate family drama, generational differences, and heartfelt pleas to prove that love can bridge any divide.

Avik, a small-town boy, comes to Kolkata chasing his dreams. Just when the city starts to overwhelm him, he meets Meghna—sharp, spirited, and nothing like him. As their lives collide, love brews in the chaos, setting off a chain of choices that could change everything.

Based on Nabarun Bhattacharya’s works, the film is set in Kolkata—a city where old traditions meet a fast-changing world. As people try to hold on to memories while facing modern realities, the story explores the push and pull between past and present in everyday lives.

Nirdharita Shilpir Anupasthitite revolves around a father and daughter, who get separated and unite after a long time. However, things take a drastic turn when the daughter has a problem adjusting to her father's rich background.

In Srijit Mukherji’s experimental drama Nirbaak, Sushmita Sen anchors four strange love stories that unfold without dialogue. A man loves a tree, a dog grieves its mistress, and a morgue attendant adores a corpse. Blurring lines between life, death, and desire—it’s love, unspoken.

When a prince crosses paths with a mysterious snake, an unexpected friendship begins. But as fate turns hostile, both are caught in a web of danger, prophecy, and betrayal. Bound by trust, they must fight forces that seek to destroy them.

Adapted from Shirshendu Mukhopadhyay’s novel, this heartfelt story follows Nayan and Shyama—two individuals brought together by love. As their relationship grows, they navigate personal struggles and societal pressures, discovering the strength of love in the face of life’s many trials.

Achintyo returns to his ancestral village with his two daughters, each carrying their own dreams, secrets, and conflicts. As they struggle to adjust to rural life, long-buried truths and unexpected turns await. What destiny has in store will test their bonds and change their lives forever.

A young bride pens heartfelt letters from her in-laws’ home, capturing her joys, struggles, and inner world. As she grows into her new life, her reflections reveal quiet strength and deep emotion. The film is based on a story by Rabindranath Tagore.

Protidwandi follows Debu, a naïve village boy who dreams of stardom and rises to fame as an actor. But as success brings power, it also awakens darkness. Will Debu hold on to his innocence—or be consumed by the very world he longed to enter?

In Proloy, teacher Barun Biswas (Parambrata Chatterjee) confronts gang rapes in Dukhiya village. After forming a protest group and demanding justice, he was murdered. Inspector Animesh Dutta (Saswata Chatterjee) returns to uncover the truth and avenge him.

Rahul (Jeet) secretly loves Puja (Chandana Sharma), but when she mistakenly believes Sumit (Jisshu Sengupta) is her admirer, Rahul remains silent. As love, friendship, and truth clash, hearts break and choices arise. Some feelings are too deep to hide forever.

A society caught between morality and corruption sets the stage for a battle of conscience. As greed, injustice, and power threaten to silence truth, a few dare to stand their ground. Will righteousness prevail, or is the system too broken?

A local rowdy falls deeply for his new neighbour –– gentle, reserved, and already promised to someone else. As love slowly grows between them, society, family, and fate close in. Prem Amaar, directed by Raj Chakraborty and starring Soham and Paayel, is a stirring tale of love against all odds.

Pratibad follows Rana, a principled man who takes on a corrupt system and deep-rooted social stigma to protect the powerless. As he faces threats, betrayal, and moral dilemmas, his resolve only grows stronger in a battle where silence is not an option.

Jeetu dreams of becoming a cop but hasn’t cleared the exams. One day, he finds a police uniform and starts pretending to be an officer. As his lies spiral out of control, Jeetu must outsmart danger and decide—will he fake it till he makes it, or crash and burn?

Uttam Kumar plays Bibhas, the estranged son of an industrialist, raised in a church, who later becomes a feared goon. Reunited with his family under the shadow of his father’s expectations, Bibhas must decide whether to reclaim his legacy or follow his own path.

Pooja and Siddhu get to know each other through common friends. Although Siddhu has a sad past, he manages to wear a smile every day. When he decides to declare his love for Pooja and ask for her hand in marriage, he faces her father, who is unhappy with Siddhu's past. What could that be?

Newly married Ranjana (Raima Sen) fears her chef husband will lose interest if she gains weight. Desperate, she joins a shady slimming clinic that promises fast results. Co-starring Parambrata, Rudranil, and Riya Sen, the film blends surreal comedy with questions of self-image.

Rehmat is forced to grow up too soon, shaped by poverty, loss, and an unforgiving world. But through every hardship, he fights to hold on to dignity and hope. The film traces his journey from struggle to strength, offering a moving portrait of resilience and the will to survive.

Shiva, separated from his family and homeland, rises as a fearless protector of the helpless. As he stands against injustice and tyranny, memories of his lost past haunt him. Will his fight for the oppressed also lead him back to the country and family he longs to reunite with?

The son of a corrupt minister runs a dangerous drug racket. When a brave student protests, he is brutally murdered. His brother, Raja, refuses to stay silent. Fueled by grief and rage, Raja takes on the powerful nexus to avenge his brother’s death and expose the truth.

Raja Babu steps in to protect Rani’s honour by accepting her and her unborn child as his own. While society raises eyebrows, Raja stands firm. But will Rani’s family—and fate—truly accept him as the father, or will his selfless act lead to unexpected consequences?

Raincoat by Rituparno Ghosh stars Ajay Devgn and Aishwarya Rai. On a rainy afternoon in Kolkata, unemployed Manoj visits Neerja, his former lover, who married for wealth. As they share memories and lies to shield their realities, a tender story of love, regret, and longing unfolds.

Abhijit Roy, a retired man, watches his three children step into adulthood with dreams of their own. But as they face life’s challenges, reality proves harsher than expected. With changing relationships, struggles, and surprises, destiny shapes each of their journeys in unexpected ways.

This heartfelt love story follows a couple who marry without romance but slowly grow to love each other. As they confront memories of their past and personal struggles, their bond deepens. Together, they learn that true love can blossom even after marriage, leaving a lasting impact.

Shatru Dhangso follows a man pushed to the edge by betrayal and loss. As he sets out to destroy those responsible, his fight becomes one of justice, sacrifice, and survival. Starring Bhagyashree, Shakil Khan, and Ilias Kanchan, this is a story where vengeance meets reckoning.

Raj, an orphan chasing his dreams, juggles college and risky stunt work to survive. Just as life starts finding rhythm, he falls for someone who makes it all feel worth it. But love comes with its own hurdles, forcing Raj to risk more than ever—for his heart, his future, and his truth.

Bijoy, a struggling singer, falls for Sonali, but hides his love to support her dreams. When tragedy strikes, Bijoy makes the ultimate sacrifice—silently. Sathi, Jeet’s breakthrough film, is a deeply emotional tale of selfless love and emotions.

Soumo marries Saptami but leaves her with a close friend to pursue higher studies. Years later, when he returns, he is shaken by a truth he never expected. What happened in his absence, and can their relationship survive the storm that follows? A gripping tale of trust, betrayal, and consequences.

Bijay falls in love with Rupa, only to discover she's the sister of his best friend, Rana. Torn between love and friendship, he chooses to sacrifice his feelings. But as emotions run deep and truths unfold, Bijay must decide if following his heart is worth risking a cherished bond.

An ailing father, burdened by worries of his declining health, fears for his family's well-being after he's gone. As time slips away, he struggles to put things in order—financially and emotionally. Will he succeed in securing a stable future for his loved ones before it's too late?

Sagar, a kind-hearted young man, falls deeply in love with Sakhi, the spirited daughter of a powerful zamindar. But their love faces fierce opposition from her authoritative father and rigid societal norms. Will their bond overcome the hurdles of class divide and parental disapproval?
The film revolves around Ranajit and Julekha belonging to rival families with old enmity. However, on their meeting, there develops an unbeatable connection that eventually turns to love.

Debabrata, deeply influenced by the teachings of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose, takes it upon himself to awaken and unite the Bengali community. As he confronts social injustice and corruption, his journey becomes one of courage, leadership, and a call to action rooted in patriotic ideals.

While Satyajit Ray’s Pather Panchali became a timeless masterpiece of Indian cinema, the boy who brought Apu to life—Subir Banerjee—faded into obscurity. This story revisits his journey, exploring how a brief but brilliant role etched him into cinematic history, yet left him largely unknown.

Raja and Priya are deeply in love, but their families stand firmly against their relationship. Caught between love and family pressure, they struggle to hold on to each other. Will they fight for their love or give in to the barriers set by society? A heartfelt tale of hope and heartbreak.

Anol falls deeply in love with Ankhi, but she doesn’t feel the same way. As he tries to win her heart, he faces rejection, self-doubt, and emotional struggle. Will Anol’s sincerity and patience make a difference, or will Ankhi’s feelings remain unchanged forever?

When Bhaskar dies in a tragic accident, his lover Priya is left heartbroken and mentally unstable. Years pass, and a man named Angshuman arrives in her village, looking just like Bhaskar. As past emotions resurface, questions arise—who is Angshuman, and why is he here?

Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar, a visionary reformer, battled deep-rooted social evils to spread education and awareness among the masses. From promoting widow remarriage to fighting caste bias, he uses knowledge as a tool for change, inspiring a new, progressive way of thinking across Bengal.

Arundhati, reeling from a series of failed relationships, is emotionally adrift when Akash enters her life with quiet strength and support. As they grow closer, healing begins—but an unexpected revelation forces them to question everything they’ve come to believe about each other.

In Swartho, Sasadhar, a simple farmer, leaves his land and stays with his nephews in the city. His nephews, expecting wealth after the land sale, are shocked when Sasadhar donates money to charity. Betrayal and greed surface as family tensions rise, forcing him to defend his values and dignity.

When Professor Manoj is falsely accused and tortured by the police, his world collapses. His daughter, refusing to stay silent, takes a bold stand for justice. As society and authorities try to silence her, she fights back—risking everything to expose the truth and restore her father's honour.

In a world of fleeting emotions, he chooses to wait—for the one he loves to see him the same way. As time tests his devotion, he holds on to hope. A tale where love doesn’t roar, but lingers quietly—until one moment, one glance, threatens to rewrite everything he thought he knew.

Ashok, a businessman, provides shelter to Mahim Halden. An ambitious Mahim kills his benefactor Ashok, and takes over his property. Ashok's son Surya comes to know the truth and makes it his life’s purpose to seek revenge from his father's killer.

This is the story of two brothers with opposite personalities—one mature and settled, the other free-spirited and dreamy. As the elder brother ties the knot, the younger begins his journey to find true love. Full of heartfelt moments and laughter, this is a warm family drama with love at its core.

This spiritual tale follows a group of Lord Shiva’s devotees whose unwavering faith is put to the test during a severe crisis. As struggles mount and hope begins to fade, they continue to pray and believe. Will their devotion be rewarded with the divine presence they seek in their darkest hour?

Zulfiqar reimagines Shakespeare’s Julius Caesar and Antony and Cleopatra in the gritty world of Kolkata’s crime syndicate. As Zulfiqar Ahmed rises in power, betrayal brews within his ranks. What follows is a gripping saga of loyalty, ambition, and revenge that shakes the underworld.

When Raghu's father commits suicide, Raghu decides to avenge his father. After serving at a juvenile home, Raghu takes a new identity in search of a new life. How far will he go to hide his past?

Vinod, a troubled student, develops intense feelings for his classmate Ria. Unaware of his inner turmoil, Ria treats him as a friend. But when she rejects his advances, Vinod’s emotions spiral into rage. Ria soon finds herself trapped in a dangerous web of obsession and violence.
